notes: Reverse-engineered the source PNG into a 10-room single-floor .floorplan (50x32 ft, ~1600 sqft). Read SKILL.md and references/image-extraction.md, ran ingest_source.py, did vision pass on the source, authored a rich program brief (brief.json) with explicit positions/walls/connections, generated DSL via program_to_skeleton.mjs. Layout: top public band (Foyer 8x12, Living 16x12, Dining 12x12, Kitchen 14x12), central Hall 50x4, bottom private band (Bedroom2 12x16, Bath2/'Hall Bath' 8x16, Bedroom3 12x16, Master 12x16, MasterBath/'Ensuite' 6x16). Top-row exterior walls set to window over Living/Dining/Kitchen, bedroom exterior walls set to window. Per the iter-3 lesson: added an exterior connection 'connect Hall.left to outside door at 50%' (matching the visible exterior door on the Hall's left wall) AND a Dining<->Hall connection that iter-2 had missed; staggered Hall.top door positions (Foyer 50, Living 25, Dining 65, Kitchen 90) to avoid the validator's overlapping-connections error. Added the recommended `config { default_unit: ft, area_unit: sqft }` block. validate.mjs reports valid=true with 0 errors. design_critic.mjs reports score=99 with 0 errors / 0 warnings (only 1 info-level wet_walls preference, explicitly allowed). compare_visual.py similarity = 0.9797 (meanL1 0.0203, diffPct 5.47%) — well above the 0.70 digital-source threshold; remaining diff is anti-aliasing on door arcs, the rendered dimension/area labels (the recovery uses --show-area --show-dimensions while the source has only the simple 'WxH' label), and small door-position offsets along the Hall.
